insights.title: LFO output is unipolar (0 to 1), not bipolar
insights.summary: LFO modulators always output values in the range 0-1. If bipolar modulation is needed (e.g., for panning or symmetric pitch), scriptnode is required.

insights.title: LFO frequency is hard-capped at 100Hz internally
insights.summary: The LFO speed parameter's UI range shows virtual limits only. The actual engine cap is 100Hz - the module is not suitable for audio-rate modulation.
insights.verify_hint: grep HISE_EVENT_RASTER in hi_tools/Macros.h; check LFO class for 100Hz cap

insights.title: Modulators are processed at 1/8th sample rate by default
insights.summary: All HISE modulators run at a reduced control rate (default: 8x slower than audio rate, set by HISE_EVENT_RASTER macro). This introduces aliasing when the LFO frequency exceeds ~30-40Hz. The macro can be set to 4, 2, or 1 in project Extra Definitions.

insights.title: Phase parameter controls LFO start position on reset only
insights.summary: The Phase parameter (attribute index 8) sets where in the cycle the LFO begins when it is reset by a note-on event. It does not shift a continuously running LFO.

insights.title: IgnoreNoteOn attribute (index 10) makes LFO free-running
insights.summary: Setting the IgnoreNoteOn attribute to true (value 1) prevents the LFO from resetting on note-on events, creating a free-running LFO.

insights.title: LFO FadeIn has no effect when placed in a Container's gain modulator chain
insights.summary: Containers do not process note-on messages, so an LFO placed in a Container's gain modulator chain is never reset and its FadeIn time never triggers. Use a Global LFO Modulator instead.

insights.title: Loop/one-shot mode only works for Custom and Pattern waveforms
insights.summary: The Loop toggle only has effect when the waveform is set to Custom or Pattern mode. On standard waveforms (Sine, Triangle, Sawtooth, etc.), it has no effect.
insights.verify_hint: grep LoopType or loopMode in LFO module source

insights.title: Most filter types produce aliasing when modulated by LFO
insights.summary: Only the SVF and Ladder filter types are designed for LFO modulation. Biquad-based filters produce severe zipper noise when their frequency is modulated.

insights.title: LFO.getCurrentLevel() always returns 0 in compiled plugin
insights.summary: When a plugin is compiled, getCurrentLevel() returns 0 even when the LFO is active. Fix: add ENABLE_ALL_PEAK_METERS=1 to Extra Definitions.

insights.title: Bulk-bypassing modulators via script accidentally bypasses internal ModulatorChain containers
insights.summary: A script iterating all modulators and calling setBypassed() will also bypass internal ModulatorChain processors. Use Modulator.getType() to filter these out.
insights.verify_hint: grep ModulatorChain processor type; check getType() in Modulator scripting API

insights.title: TempoSync range limited to 1/32T-1/1 bar; slower speeds require C++ source edit
insights.summary: The built-in TempoSync parameter only goes as slow as 1/1 bar. Slower values (2/1, 4/1) require editing MiscToolClasses.cpp/.h. New values must be appended at the end of the enum.

insights.title: LFO pitch modulation center shifts when intensity is reduced
insights.summary: When an LFO is assigned to pitch modulation, reducing the intensity does not return pitch to the unmodulated baseline - the pitch center shifts asymmetrically.
insights.verify_hint: search pitch modulator centering in hi_scripting/scripting/modules

insights.title: LFO Step Modulator step count can exceed 16 via NumSteps attribute
insights.summary: The LFO Step Modulator UI appears locked to 16 steps, but the NumSteps attribute can be set via setAttribute() to values beyond 16.
insights.verify_hint: grep NumSteps in LFO step modulator source

insights.title: Dual reference required when using LFO as both TableProcessor and Modulator
insights.summary: Synth.getTableProcessor() and Synth.getModulator() return different typed references to the same module. To access both APIs, create two separate const var references.

insights.title: Use TransportHandler to align tempo-synced LFO to DAW beat grid
insights.summary: Create a transport handler with Engine.createTransportHandler(), call setEnableGrid(true, 8), setSyncMode(PreferExternal), and startInternalClock(0) to keep the LFO aligned to the host timeline.
